critic_raw: The evaluation looks potentially contaminated because of the heavy oversampling of rare positives combined with nested cross-validation. If augmented images from a positive example bleed into the validation split — even partially via the cross-validation index — the model's measured recall would be inflated on those near-duplicates. The risk is higher with aggressive augmentations (rotations, flips, brightness) that produce images visually very similar to the original.
defender_raw: The pipeline description is explicit: splits are performed at the original-image level before any augmentation; augmentations are generated on the fly inside the training fold only. This means augmented images cannot bleed into validation by construction — they are created during the training pass on training-fold images and never exist as discrete files that could be erroneously indexed into validation. The later-week test uses only raw production images, providing an additional contamination barrier. The claim is limited to this specific production setting.

debate_rounds.critic: The split-before-augment and fold-local augmentation combination does prevent the specific leakage pathway I was concerned about. On-the-fly augmentation in the training loop is clean by design if the implementation is correct. I accept the defender's argument. The only remaining point is whether the part-level grouping audit was complete — that is a verification concern, not an established problem. I concede the methodology as described is sound.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. The audit question is the appropriate verification step. The methodology is sound as described.

adjudication_raw: The critic fully concedes after one round. The fold-local on-the-fly augmentation design and split-before-augment order together prevent the leakage pathway. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: augmentation leakage in nested CV — fully conceded when fold-local design acknowledged

empirical_test.condition: Pipeline audit confirms split assignment precedes augmentation and augmentation is fold-local; part grouping audit finds no same-physical-unit boundary crossing; final test uses raw later-week images
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Any augmented or near-duplicate image from the same original unit appears in validation or test splits
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All checks confirm: fold assignment first, augmentation inside training loop only, raw later-week test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Core leakage checks pass but part grouping for some edge-case units is incompletely documented
